title: Add trust-flush CronJob sub-reconciler
summary: Review of changes addressing external review #1 feedback from berendt. Two fixes applied: (1) ptr.To() for Suspend field to avoid pointer aliasing live CR state (W-002), (2) Chainsaw E2E test suite added under tests/e2e/keystone/trust-flush/ covering CronJob creation, schedule/suspend assertions, manual job trigger, condition progression, CronJob deletion on disable, and condition update to TrustFlushNotRequired (W-001). A new review pattern file documents the ptr.To() lesson. All changes are minimal, correctly scoped, and consistent with existing codebase patterns.

tests_checklist.item: Unit tests exist and pass (18 controller tests, 4 webhook tests)
E2E tests added for trust-flush feature (chainsaw-test.yaml)
E2E test covers CronJob creation when spec.trustFlush is set
E2E test covers CronJob deletion when spec.trustFlush is removed
E2E test covers TrustFlushReady condition progression
E2E test covers manual job trigger and completion
E2E test verifies Ready=True maintained after job execution
Edge cases covered (nil trustFlush, deletion of existing CronJob, error propagation)

code_quality_checklist.item: ptr.To() usage matches codebase convention (security_context.go, integration_test.go)
No remaining &struct.Field patterns for value types in reconcile_trustflush.go
Review pattern file follows established format

security_checklist.item: No secrets exposed in E2E test fixtures

architecture_checklist.item: E2E test follows Chainsaw pattern (SPDX header, JMESPath conditions, numbered steps, timeouts.assert: 5m)
Fixture files use 00-/01- numeric prefix ordering
Unique CR name (keystone-trust-flush) for parallel test execution
Deletion assertion uses script pattern consistent with network-policy E2E test

dry_yagni_checklist.item: Changes are minimal and focused on addressing review feedback

fail_fast_checklist.item: ptr.To() creates safe copy preventing deferred pointer aliasing

defensive_checklist.item: E2E catch blocks include comprehensive diagnostics (pod logs, events, job status)
